title: Birth of the Prophet (صَلَّى ٱللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ)
description: Born in Makkah (12 or 17 Rabi' al-Awwal).

title: First Revelation
description: The Quran began descending in the Cave of Hira (Ramadan 27).

title: The Hijrah
description: Migration to Madinah; established the first Islamic state.

title: Battle of Badr
description: First major victory (17 Ramadan); change of Qibla to Makkah (15 Sha'ban).

title: Change of Qibla to Makkah
description: Change of Qibla to Makkah from Masjid al-Aqsa in the mosque of Qiblatain.

title: Battle of Uhud
description: Major trial for early Muslims; martyrdom of Hamza (رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ).

title: Battle of the Trench
description: Defending Madinah against the confederates. During the Battle of the Trench, Ali ibn Abi Talib secured a decisive psychological and military victory by crossing the moat to face and defeat the legendary, unbeatable giant Amr ibn Abd Wudd in single combat.

title: Treaty of Hudaybiyyah
description: Strategic peace treaty with the Quraysh. The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah was a strategic masterpiece where the Prophet Muhammad (صَلَّى ٱللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ), with Ali ibn Abi Talib (كَرَّمَ اللهُ وَجْهَهُ الْكَرِيم) serving as the official scribe, secured a ten-year truce that transitioned the Muslims from a persecuted group to a recognized political power.

title: Victory at Khaybar
description: The Victory at Khaybar was secured when the Prophet Muhammad (صَلَّى ٱللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ) entrusted the banner to Ali ibn Abi Talib (كَرَّمَ اللهُ وَجْهَهُ الْكَرِيم), who miraculously breached the impenetrable gates of the Al-Qamus fortress and defeated the formidable warrior Marhab. (Safar/Dhul-Hijjah).

title: Conquest of Makkah
description: Bloodless liberation and cleansing of the Kaaba (20 Ramadan). During the Conquest of Mecca, the Prophet Muhammad (صَلَّى ٱللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ) entered the city in a display of mercy and triumph, where Ali ibn Abi Talib (كَرَّمَ اللهُ وَجْهَهُ الْكَرِيم) famously climbed onto the Prophet's shoulders to reach and destroy the idols within the Kaaba, permanently restoring it to the worship of one God.

title: Farewell Pilgrimage
description: The Prophet (صَلَّى ٱللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ)’s final Hajj and Final Sermon (9 Dhul-Hijjah) During the Farewell Pilgrimage, the Prophet (صَلَّى ٱللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ) delivered his Final Sermon at Mount Arafat, establishing the core principles of human rights, equality, and the sanctity of life and property.

title: Death of the Prophet (صَلَّى ٱللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ)
description: Passing of Prophet Muhammad (صَلَّى ٱللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ); Abu Bakr (رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ) becomes 1st Caliph (12 Rabi' al-Awwal).

title: Death of Abu Bakr (رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ)
description: Passing of the 1st Caliph; Umar (رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ) becomes 2nd Caliph (Jumada al-Akhira).

title: Conquest of Jerusalem (Umar)
description: Muslims enter Jerusalem under Caliph Umar (رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ) (Rabi' al-Awwal).

title: Battle of the Camel
description: First civil conflict during Caliph Ali (كَرَّمَ اللهُ وَجْهَهُ الْكَرِيم)'s reign (10 Jumada al-Akhira).

title: Tragedy of Karbala
description: The Martyrdom of Husayn ibn Ali (رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهَا) at the Battle of Karbala stands as a supreme sacrifice for justice and truth, where the Prophet’s grandson gave his life including his family members and 72 followes to preserve the core values of Islam against tyranny.

title: Conquest of Jerusalem (Saladin)
description: On the 27th of Rajab, 583 AH, Saladin recaptured the holy city of Jerusalem after 88 years of Crusader rule, notably choosing to enter on the anniversary of the Prophet (صَلَّى ٱللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ)'s Night Journey to show mercy and magnanimity toward the defeated inhabitants.

title: Battle of Ain Jalut
description: The Battle of Ain Jalut on the 25th of Ramadan, 648 AH, marked a monumental turning point in history as the Mamluk forces handed the Mongol Empire its first major permanent defeat, effectively halting their westward expansion into the Islamic world.

title: Fall of Constantinople
description: On the 20th of Jumada al-Akhira, 857 AH, the twenty-one-year-old Sultan Mehmed II (al-Fatih) achieved the historic conquest of Constantinople, fulfilling a centuries-old prophecy and transforming the Ottoman State into a global empire.

title: Islamic New Year
description: The first day of Muharram marks the beginning of the new Hijri year.

title: Day of Ashura
description: 10th of Muharram – commemorates Musa alaihis salam's salvation and Husayn (رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهَا)'s martyrdom including 72 of his followers.

title: Mawlid al-Nabi
description: The Mawlid al-Nabi is celebrated on the 12th of Rabi' al-Awwal (according to the majority of Sunni scholars) or the 17th of Rabi' al-Awwal (according to Shia tradition), serving as a global day of reflection, poetry, and charity to honor the birth and mercy of the Prophet Muhammad (صَلَّى ٱللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ).

title: Isra and Mi'raj
description: For the Isra and Mi'raj, traditionally commemorated on the 27th of Rajab, the Prophet Muhammad (صَلَّى ٱللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ) was miraculously transported from Mecca to Jerusalem and then ascended through the heavens to the Divine Presence.

title: Laylat al-Bara'ah
description: The Night of Mid-Sha'ban (Laylat al-Bara'ah), occurring on the night of 14th of Sha'ban, is a significant time of spiritual reflection when Allah (سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَىٰ) descends to the lowest heaven to grant widespread forgiveness to all who seek it, as narrated in several ahadith by the Prophet Muhammad (صَلَّى ٱللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ).

title: Start of Ramadan
description: The blessed month of Ramadan begins on the 1st of Ramadan, marking the start of a period of fasting, prayer, and deep spiritual reflection as it commemorates the month in which the Quran was first revealed to the Prophet Muhammad (صَلَّى ٱللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ).

title: Laylat al-Qadr
description: Laylat al-Qadr, the Night of Power, occurs during the last ten nights of Ramadan (most commonly sought on the 27th night) and marks the auspicious occasion when the Quran was first sent down to the Prophet Muhammad (صَلَّى ٱللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ), making worship on this single night superior to that of a thousand months.

title: Eid al-Fitr
description: Eid al-Fitr, celebrated on the 1st of Shawwal, is the joyous festival of breaking the fast that marks the completion of Ramadan, where Muslims gather for congregational prayers, give Zakat al-Fitr to the needy, and express gratitude to Allah (سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَىٰ) for the strength to observe the holy month.

title: Day of Arafat
description: The Day of Arafah, occurring on the 9th of Dhul-Hijjah, is the most critical day of Hajj when the Prophet Muhammad (صَلَّى ٱللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ) stated Hajj is Arafah, marking a time of immense forgiveness where Allah (سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَىٰ) descends to the lowest heaven to boast of His servants to the angels.

title: Eid al-Adha
description: Eid al-Adha, celebrated on the 10th of Dhul-Hijjah, is the joyous festival of sacrifice that honors the profound devotion of Ibrahim (عَلَيْهِ ٱلسَّلَامُ) and serves as a time for Muslims to perform the Qurbani and share meat with the needy to express gratitude to Allah (سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَىٰ).

title: Death of Abu Bakr as-Siddiq
description: The first Caliph, Abu Bakr al-Siddiq (رَضِيَ ٱللَّٰهُ عَنْهُ), passed away on the 22nd or 23rd of Jumada al-Thani, 13 AH, after a short illness, leaving behind a legacy of unwavering loyalty to the Prophet Muhammad (صَلَّى ٱللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ) and the foundational stability of the early Islamic state.

title: Death of Umar ibn al-Khattab
description: The second Caliph, Umar ibn al-Khattab (رَضِيَ ٱللَّٰهُ عَنْهُ), was martyred on the 26th of Dhul-Hijjah, 23 AH, after being fatally struck by Abu Lu'lu'ah while leading the Fajr prayer, marking the loss of a leader renowned for his justice and expansion of the Islamic state.

title: Death of Uthman ibn Affan
description: The third Caliph, Uthman ibn Affan (رَضِيَ ٱللَّٰهُ عَنْهُ), was martyred on the 18th of Dhul-Hijjah (though some sources cite the 12th), 35 AH, following a prolonged siege of his house, leaving a legacy defined by the final compilation of the Holy Quran.

title: Death of Ali ibn Abi Talib
description: The fourth Caliph, Ali ibn Abi Talib (كَرَّمَ اللهُ وَجْهَهُ الْكَرِيم), was martyred on the 21st of Ramadan, 40 AH, after being struck by a poisoned sword while in prayer on 19th Ramadhan, leaving behind a legacy of unmatched wisdom, courage, and justice.

title: Death of Imam Abu Hanifa
description: The founder of the Hanafi school of jurisprudence, Imam Abu Hanifa (رَحِمَهُ ٱللَّٰهُ), passed away in the month of Rajab (or alternatively Sha'ban), 150 AH, in Baghdad, leaving behind the most widely followed legal methodology in the Islamic world.

title: Death of Imam Malik
description: The founder of the Maliki school of jurisprudence, Imam Malik ibn Anas (رَحِمَهُ ٱللَّٰهُ), passed away on the 11th of Rabi' al-Awwal, 179 AH, in the city of Medina, leaving behind his monumental work, the Muwatta, and a legacy of deep adherence to the traditions of the Prophet Muhammad (صَلَّى ٱللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ).

title: Death of Imam al-Shafi'i
description: The founder of the Shafi'i school of jurisprudence, Imam al-Shafi'i (رَحِمَهُ ٱللَّٰهُ), passed away on the 30th of Rajab, 204 AH, in Fustat, Egypt, leaving behind a profound legacy as the Architect of Islamic Jurisprudence through his foundational work, al-Risala.

title: Death of Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal
description: The founder of the Hanbali school of jurisprudence, Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal (رَحِمَهُ ٱللَّٰهُ), passed away on the 12th of Rabi' al-Awwal, 241 AH, in Baghdad, leaving a legacy of immense steadfastness in the face of trial and a profound devotion to the Sunnah of the Prophet Muhammad (صَلَّى ٱللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ).

title: Death of Imam al-Bukhari
description: Compiler of Sahih al-Bukhari, died 256 AH.

title: Death of Imam Muslim
description: Compiler of Sahih Muslim, died 261 AH (25 Rajab).

title: Death of Imam Abu Dawood
description: Compiler of Sunan Abu Dawood, died 275 AH.

title: Death of Imam at-Tirmidhi
description: Compiler of Jami' at-Tirmidhi, died 279 AH.

title: Death of Imam an-Nasa'i
description: Compiler of Sunan an-Nasa'i, died 303 AH.

title: Death of Imam Ibn Majah
description: Compiler of Sunan Ibn Majah, died 273 AH.

title: Death of Hasan ibn Ali
description: Imam Hasan ibn Ali (عَلَيْهِ ٱلسَّلَامُ وَرَضِيَ ٱللَّٰهُ عَنْهُ) was martyred after being poisoned by his wife Ja'da bint al-Ash'ath, an act widely attributed to the instigation of Mu'awiya I

title: Death of Ali Zayn al-Abidin
description: Imam Ali al-Sajjad (عَلَيْهِ ٱلسَّلَامُ وَرَضِيَ ٱللَّٰهُ عَنْهُ) is reported by Shia authorities to have been martyred by poison on the 25th of Muharram, likely on the orders of the Umayyad caliph al-Walid.

title: Death of Muhammad al-Baqir
description: Imam Muhammad al-Baqir (عَلَيْهِ ٱلسَّلَامُ وَرَضِيَ ٱللَّٰهُ عَنْهُ) was martyred on the 7th of Dhul-Hijjah after being poisoned by the Umayyad caliph Hisham ibn Abd al-Malik, often reported through a poisoned saddle.

title: Death of Ja'far al-Sadiq
description: Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (عَلَيْهِ ٱلسَّلَامُ وَرَضِيَ ٱللَّٰهُ عَنْهُ) was martyred on the 25th of Shawwal after being poisoned at the instigation of the Abbasid caliph al-Mansur, frequently cited as involving poisoned grapes or water.

title: Death of Musa al-Kadhim
description: Imam Musa al-Kadhim (عَلَيْهِ ٱلسَّلَامُ وَرَضِيَ ٱللَّٰهُ عَنْهُ) was martyred on the 25th of Rajab, 183 AH, after being poisoned with dates while imprisoned in Baghdad on the orders of the Abbasid caliph Harun al-Rashid.

title: Death of Ali al-Rida
description: Imam Ali al-Rida (عَلَيْهِ ٱلسَّلَامُ وَرَضِيَ ٱللَّٰهُ عَنْهُ) was martyred on the last day of Safar, 203 AH, after being poisoned with grapes or pomegranate juice while in Tus on the orders of the Abbasid caliph al-Ma'mun.

title: Death of Muhammad al-Taqi
description: Imam Muhammad al-Taqi (عَلَيْهِ ٱلسَّلَامُ وَرَضِيَ ٱللَّٰهُ عَنْهُ), also known as al-Jawad, was martyred on the last day of Dhul-Qi'dah, 220 AH, after being poisoned in Baghdad at the instigation of the Abbasid caliph al-Mu'tasim.

title: Death of Ali al-Naqi
description: Imam Ali al-Naqi (كَرَّمَ اللهُ وَجْهَهُ الْكَرِيم وَعَلَيْهِ ٱلسَّلَامُ وَرَضِيَ ٱللَّٰهُ عَنْهُ) was martyred on the 3rd of Rajab, 254 AH, in Samarra, after being poisoned at the instigation of the Abbasid caliph al-Mu'tazz.

title: Death of Hasan al-Askari
description: Imam Hasan al-Askari (عَلَيْهِ ٱلسَّلَامُ وَرَضِيَ ٱللَّٰهُ عَنْهُ) was martyred on the 8th of Rabi' al-Awwal, 260 AH, in Samarra, after being poisoned on the orders of the Abbasid caliph al-Mu'tamid, marking the final public leadership before the period of Occultation began.

title: Martyrdom of Sayyiduna Ammar ibn Yasir (Radi Allahu 'anhu)
description: Sayyiduna Ammar ibn Yasir (Radi Allahu 'anhu) was martyred at the age of about 94 by the rebel group while fighting on the side of Hazrat Ali (Karramallahu wajhahul karim) in the Battle of Siffin on the 9th of Safar, 37 AH.

title: Birth of Imam Ja'far as-Sadiq (Radi Allahu 'anhu)
description: Imam Ja'far as-Sadiq (Radi Allahu 'anhu) was born in Medina on the 17th of Rabi' al-Awwal, 80 AH. He is recognized as a great jurist in Islamic history and one of the pioneers of modern science and philosophy.

title: Birth of Hasan al-Askari (Radi Allahu 'anhu)
description: Imam Hasan al-Askari (Radi Allahu 'anhu) was born in the holy city of Medina on the 8th of Rabi' al-Thani, 232 AH. He was the eleventh Imam of Islam and is admired for his profound knowledge and spiritual leadership.

title: Day of Ayat al-Mubahala
description: The Day of Mubahala is observed on the 24th of Dhu al-Hijjah in the Hijri calendar, commemorating the challenge to prove the truth between the Prophet Muhammad (Sallallahu 'alayhi wa alihi wa sallam) and the Christians of Najran, as well as the special status of the Ahl al-Bayt.

title: Birth of Imam Zayn al-Abidin (Radi Allahu 'anhu)
description: Imam Zayn al-Abidin (Radi Allahu 'anhu) was born in Medina on the 5th of Sha'ban, 38 AH. He remains immortal in Islamic history for his unparalleled worship, patience, and the collection of supplications known as 'Sahifa al-Sajjadiyya'.

title: Birth of Sayyida Fatima Zahra (Salamullahi 'alayha)
description: Sayyida Fatima Zahra (Salamullahi 'alayha) was born in Mecca on the 20th of Jumada al-Thani, in the fifth year of Prophethood. She is the beloved daughter of the Prophet (Sallallahu 'alayhi wa alihi wa sallam), the leader of the women of Paradise, and the highest ideal of womanhood in Islamic history.

title: Birth of Muhammad al-Baqir (Radi Allahu 'anhu)
description: Imam Muhammad al-Baqir (Radi Allahu 'anhu) was born in Medina on the 1st of Rajab, 57 AH. He was the fifth Imam of Islam and is well known as 'Baqir al-'Ulum' (the one who opens knowledge) for his profound wisdom and unveiling of sciences.

title: Birth of Imam Ali al-Hadi (an-Naqi) (Radi Allahu 'anhu)
description: Imam Ali al-Hadi (an-Naqi) (Radi Allahu 'anhu) was born in Medina on the 15th of Dhu al-Hijjah, 212 AH. He is renowned for his deep scholarship, spiritual leadership, and for spreading the correct teachings of Islam even under the adverse conditions of the Abbasid rule.

title: Demise of Hazrat Khawja Abu Talib (Radi Allahu 'anhu)
description: Hazrat Khawja Abu Talib (Radi Allahu 'anhu) passed away in Mecca on the 26th or 28th of Rajab in the tenth year of Prophethood. He was the beloved uncle and guardian of the Prophet (Sallallahu 'alayhi wa alihi wa sallam), his main protector and caretaker from childhood through the difficult times of spreading Islam.

title: Birth of Imam Hussain (Radi Allahu 'anhu)
description: Imam Hussain (Radi Allahu 'anhu) was born in Medina on the 3rd of Sha'ban, 4 AH. He is the beloved grandson of the Prophet (Sallallahu 'alayhi wa alihi wa sallam) and known throughout the world as the great martyr of Karbala for his uncompromising struggle for truth and justice.

title: Birth of Hazrat Khawja Abu Talib (Radi Allahu 'anhu)
description: Hazrat Khawja Abu Talib (Radi Allahu 'anhu) was born in Mecca on the 29th of Shawwal. He was the beloved uncle and guardian of the Prophet (Sallallahu 'alayhi wa alihi wa sallam), his main protector and caretaker from childhood through the difficult times of spreading Islam.

title: Birth of Imam Ali ibn Musa ar-Ridha (Radi Allahu 'anhu)
description: Imam Ali ibn Musa ar-Ridha (Radi Allahu 'anhu) was born in Medina on the 11th of Dhu al-Qi'dah, 148 AH. He is the eighth Imam of Islam, a great ascetic and spiritual guide, adorned with the title 'Aalim-e Aal-e Muhammad' (the scholar of the family of Muhammad).

title: Birth of Imam Ali Naqi (Radi Allahu 'anhu)
description: Imam Ali Naqi (Radi Allahu 'anhu) was born on the 15th of Dhu al-Hijjah, 212 AH. As the tenth Imam, he guided the believers despite extreme adversity and gifted the Ummah with profound supplications such as 'Ziyarat Jami'ah Kabirah'.

title: Proclamation of the Wilayah of Imam Ali (Karramallahu wajhahul karim)
description: Eid al-Ghadir (Proclamation of the Mastership of the Master of the Universe): On the 18th of Dhu al-Hijjah, 10 AH, at a place called Ghadir Khumm, the Prophet Muhammad (Sallallahu 'alayhi wa alihi wa sallam), by Allah's command, declared Hazrat Ali (Karramallahu wajhahul karim) as the Mawla (guardian) of the believers.
